text:
Team Canada athletes will be outfitted by Lululemon at the 2024 Olympic and Paralympic Games in Paris this summer.ГӮ

The gear the athletes will wear during the opening ceremony, podium, media appearances and closing ceremony was revealed by Lululemon on Tuesday. The brand will outfit Team Canada for the second time in the Olympics and for the first time during the Summer Games.ГӮ

Take a look.ГӮ

This is the gear that Team Canada athletes will be wearing in Paris at the 2024 Olympics and Paralympics Г°ВҹВҮВЁГ°ВҹВҮВҰpic.twitter.com/LKoIV6q1Y0

Olympic and Paralympic athletes were involved in the design and testing process of the gear, allowing for more adaptable and customizable features to be incorporated. Some of those innovations include magnetic close zippers and more accessible pockets for Paralympians along with jackets and pants that can quickly convert to short sleeves and shorts in the warmer weather in Paris.

Tennis player Leylah Fernandez was among the athletes who revealed the red-and-white-emblazoned gear in Toronto on Tuesday. She is most excited by how the gear represents Canada and the pride the athletes hold for their country.ГӮ

ГўВҖВңOnce I saw the kit, I was like, ГўВҖВҳWow, this is it,ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВқ Fernandez told SI. ГўВҖВңHopefully we do well and hopefully we are able to bring back medals so that we can showcase this kit a lot more and just show that Canada is an awesome country.ГўВҖВқГӮ

Wheelchair rugby player Zak Madell was one of the Paralympians who helped test the gear for nearly two years and provided feedback on improvements to be made. He is most appreciative of the accessibility of the final product and how it fits on his body.

ГўВҖВңA lot of thought, research, even insight from the Paralympic athletes went into this," Madell said. "Seeing our insight from the conversations I had a couple years ago really coming to fruition and seeing it as part of the kit has been a really good experience.ГўВҖВқ

It was important to the Lululemon team to include Madell and others to test the gear and create a collection that met the athletesГўВҖВҷ needs. For Lululemon chief product officer Sun Choe, that meant including small but meaningful detailsГўВҖВ”like a fidget-spinner-type piece on the cord of a bucket hat or words of encouragement printed in text and braille on the inside of jackets to ease nerves that may arise.ГӮ

Ultimately, the collectionГўВҖВҷs designsГўВҖВ”inspired by the ГўВҖВҳCity of Light ГўВҖВҷГўВҖВ”are reflective of efforts to make the Team Canada kit for the upcoming summer Games inclusive and adaptable throughout various situations to all who wear it.

ГўВҖВңSo proud of what weГўВҖВҷve been able to do to kind of solve the emotional pressure, as well as making sure that we had adaptable clothes that really worked for the Paralympians,ГўВҖВқ Choe said.

The gear will be seen in action this summer when the athletes enter the opening ceremony in Paris on Friday, July 26 and throughout the Olympic and Paralympic Games.ГӮ
